J1.basis: Lumina is mid-pivot from a product called InboxZap (email cleanup) to an AI meeting-notes assistant... inboxzap-branded queries still rank OK (position 4–9)... but every topical query... has cratered to position 40–60 with near-zero clicks despite huge impressions (300–3900)
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot and grounds it in real GSC positions/impressions, framing inbox-cleanup demand as legacy and the metadata mismatch as the cause. Loses a point only because the framing is diagnostic rather than carried through every recommendation.

J2.basis: app/sitemap.ts — now includes all blog post URLs dynamically... app/blog/page.tsx — converted to an async Server Component, rendering real <Link href> per post
J2.rationale: No per-asset disposition for the 12 legacy posts — it blanket re-surfaces ALL of them via sitemap and internal links with no harvest/redirect/sunset decision and no citation of individual post impressions/positions, which is the opposite of a data-justified inventory walk.

J3.basis: kept a "(formerly InboxZap)" mention to preserve the residual branded-search equity shown in GSC rather than discarding it outright
J3.rationale: Avoids torching equity (preserves branded mention, fixes positioning) but leans toward the other failure mode by wholesale re-exposing all legacy inbox-cleanup posts with no sequenced transition plan or meeting-assistant content cluster; the tension is never explicitly articulated or resolved.

J1.basis: **Lumina is mid-pivot** from a product called InboxZap (email cleanup) to an AI meeting-notes assistant... Google is indexing a page whose title/description contradict its content
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot, cross-references GSC vs. source, correctly reads inbox queries as legacy demand and the title/meta mismatch as the cause, and frames the fixes around the new positioning. Loses a point only because the legacy-vs-new distinction drives on-page fixes but not the content strategy.

J2.basis: **`app/sitemap.ts`** — now includes all blog post URLs dynamically instead of the stubbed static list
J2.rationale: No per-asset harvest/redirect/sunset dispositions and no citation of individual post impressions/positions; it treats all 12 legacy posts as an undifferentiated bundle to re-expose, which is nearly the opposite of a migration plan.

J3.basis: kept a "(formerly InboxZap)" mention to preserve the residual branded-search equity shown in GSC rather than discarding it outright
J3.rationale: Preserves branded equity and builds the meeting-assistant positioning (avoids torching everything), but by re-exposing all inbox-cleanup posts to crawlers with no disposition or sequencing it drifts toward re-chasing legacy content and never articulates the transition tension explicitly.

J1.basis: Lumina is mid-pivot from a product called InboxZap (email cleanup) to an AI meeting-notes assistant... `inboxzap`-branded queries still rank OK... but every topical query... has cratered to position 40–60
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot, correctly reads GSC as legacy inbox demand vs. the live meeting-assistant product, and frames the diagnosis around it. Falls just short of anchor_10 because it doesn't note the repo being partially stale as a distinct 'reality' beyond the title-tag bug.

J2.basis: `app/sitemap.ts` — now includes all blog post URLs dynamically instead of the stubbed static list
J2.rationale: No per-asset disposition: the 12 legacy inbox posts are cited only in aggregate (impressions 300–3900, pos 40–60) and are all blanket-resurfaced into the sitemap/blog index — the opposite of a harvest/301/keep/sunset decision grounded in each post's numbers.

J3.basis: kept a '(formerly InboxZap)' mention to preserve the residual branded-search equity shown in GSC rather than discarding it outright
J3.rationale: Preserves branded equity and builds the meeting-assistant framing (good middle-ground instinct), but re-exposing every legacy inbox-cleanup post wholesale with no transition or repositioning plan leans toward doubling down on demand for a product that no longer exists; the tension is never articulated or sequenced.
